structural_verification.evidence: OMS consumption actions fire while muted (no ~oms_muted guard). OMSViewChange wipes consumed effects. Fix adds ~oms_muted[p] guard to all 3 consumption actions.

proposal.technique_name: reactive constraint enforcement on state change
proposal.claim: OMS consumption actions lack ~oms_muted guard, allowing cursor advancement while muted; OMSViewChange then wipes oms_log, losing effects that archivers may not have ingested yet, breaking OMS archival completeness.

review.severity_assessment: medium — effects not globally lost (exist in CE/OB archivers), but OMS archival path has a gap; no explicit OMS archival completeness invariant in spec
review.critical_interaction: OMSViewChange cursor computation at line 516 uses post-consumption cursor as starting offset; effects consumed-but-wiped are unreachable in new session
review.fix_assessment: minimal and correct — ~oms_muted[p] guard blocks consumption during recovery window; OMSViewChange is the designed catch-up path

objections.detail: No explicit OMS archival completeness property in spec; consequence is semantic incorrectness, not a numbered invariant violation. Severity is medium not high.

judgment.ruling: Bug is real. Fix is minimal and correct. Reviewer's overstatement objection noted but does not change ruling — muted OMS should not consume; spec should enforce this.
judgment.fix_correctness: Adding ~oms_muted[p] as precondition guard to all three consumption actions blocks consumption during recovery window. No liveness risk: WF on OMSUnmute and archiver ingest actions ensures muted period terminates.
judgment.state_space_impact: reduces — three actions disabled during muted period
